<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot on 2026-03-30 -->
# Plan: Baoh Assistant - Batched Discovery Questions with Follow-Up Tracking

## Objective
Change discovery from one-question-at-a-time to a batched model where the discovery agent asks up to 5 initial questions at once, then asks targeted follow-ups in up to 2 rounds (max 5 follow-up questions total), while preserving existing ContactCollection and Email steps.

## Source
- User-confirmed decisions captured on 2026-03-30.

## Confirmed Decisions
1. Keep ContactCollection and Email steps.
2. Ask up to 5 initial discovery questions based on confidence/known fields.
3. Cap applies separately:
   - Initial discovery questions: max 5.
   - Follow-up questions: max 5 total.
4. Follow-up cap is session-wide total (not per initial question).
5. If user answers only a subset, ask only unresolved items next round.
6. One user answer may map to multiple question records.
7. Follow-up questions are linked to a parent initial question.
8. Track question kind as Initial/FollowUp and include round number.
9. Persist both asked-question records and answered discovery records as separate records.
10. Keep incomplete asked logs when user abandons or partially responds.
11. Dedup scope is current session asked list only.
12. Enforce non-overlap within the same initial batch before sending to user.
13. If user response covers unasked relevant areas, agent may skip follow-ups.
14. Refine RequestType after each user reply.
15. Discovery is considered complete when all are true:
   - Required discovery fields are complete, or follow-up rounds are exhausted.
   - Explicit user confirmation is received.
   - Any additional user details are stored in "other" field(s).
16. If follow-up limits are reached with unresolved critical gaps, summarizer includes an "unknowns" section.
17. User replies should use deterministic numbered format (for example, 1-5).
18. Summarizer output should contain distilled findings only (no question lineage in final narrative).

## Durable Architecture Decisions
- Workflow remains multi-agent handoff:
  - `introduction_agent` -> `discovery_agent` -> `contact_collection_agent` -> `summariser_agent` -> `email_agent`
- MCP boundaries remain unchanged:
  - Agent orchestrates only; persistence lives in `InterviewCoach.Mcp.PresaleData`.
- Discovery control state becomes explicit and persisted (to avoid ambiguous routing):
  - Track pending unresolved discovery items and follow-up progress at lead level.
  - Maintain an internal discovery completeness score for diagnostics and follow-up tuning.
- Question logs remain dual-track:
  - Asked log for prompts sent.
  - Discovery Q&A log for captured answers.

## Data Contract Updates

### Lead-level additions (Presale state)
Add persisted fields to support deterministic batched routing:
- `InitialDiscoveryAskedCount` (int, 0-5)
- `FollowUpAskedCount` (int, 0-5)
- `CurrentDiscoveryRound` (int, 0-2)
- `PendingDiscoveryItemsJson` (serialized unresolved item keys, or normalized table)
- `AdditionalNotes` (string, stores extra information not mapped to canonical fields)
- `DiscoveryUserConfirmed` (bool)
- `DiscoveryCompletenessScore` (decimal 0.00-1.00, diagnostics only)
- `DiscoveryCompletenessReason` (string/json explaining missing or satisfied topic coverage)

### Asked question metadata additions
Extend asked-question record with:
- `QuestionKind` (`Initial` | `FollowUp`)
- `RoundNumber` (0 for initial, 1-2 for follow-ups)
- `ParentAskedQuestionId` (nullable Guid; required for follow-ups)
- `QuestionTopicKey` (string; supports overlap checks and unresolved targeting)

### Discovery Q&A additions
Extend discovery answer record with:
- `AskedQuestionId` (Guid, link to asked log)
- `QuestionKind` (`Initial` | `FollowUp`)
- `RoundNumber` (0-2)
- `ParentAskedQuestionId` (nullable Guid)
- `CoverageTopicKeys` (string list/json; supports one answer covering multiple items)

## Discovery State Machine

### States
- `DiscoveryActive`
- `DiscoveryAwaitingConfirmation`
- `ContactCollection`
- `Completed`

### Entry
On discovery handoff:
- Load lead + asked log + discovery Q&A.
- Recompute unresolved topics.
- Determine round and remaining quotas.

### Initial batch behavior
- Ask up to 5 initial questions based on known gaps and confidence.
- Persist all asked questions immediately as asked-log records (kind=`Initial`, round=0).
- Enforce non-overlap within the same batch before send.
- Provide numbered response template for the user.

### Reply ingestion behavior
After each user reply:
- Parse numbered answers deterministically.
- Map each answer to one or many asked questions.
- Persist discovery Q&A records.
- Update unresolved topics.
- Recompute and persist `DiscoveryCompletenessScore` and `DiscoveryCompletenessReason`.
- Refine `RequestType`.
- Persist any extra unmapped details in `AdditionalNotes`.

### Follow-up behavior
- Allowed only for unresolved items.
- Maximum 2 follow-up rounds.
- Maximum 5 follow-up asked questions total across session.
- Each follow-up must reference a parent initial question.
- Persist asked log before user answers.

### Discovery completion gate
Discovery can complete only when:
- Required discovery fields are complete OR follow-up rounds exhausted.
- Explicit user confirmation is captured.

Fallback behavior (anti-stall, now defined):
- If explicit confirmation is still unavailable after 2 direct confirmation prompts, OR after 2 consecutive partially responsive turns,
  discovery must auto-advance to `ContactCollection` to prevent workflow stall.
- In this fallback path:
  - Persist `DiscoveryUserConfirmed = false`.
  - Persist unresolved critical topics in `PendingDiscoveryItemsJson`.
  - Persist `DiscoveryCompletenessReason` including `confirmation_unavailable`.
  - Preserve all partial discovery details in `AdditionalNotes`.
- Summarizer must use these persisted unresolved signals to populate `unknowns` when needed.

Notes:
- `DiscoveryCompletenessScore` is diagnostic only and must not be shown to end users.
- Score supports follow-up prioritization and unknowns threshold tuning, but it does not replace the explicit completion gate.

Then:
- Handoff to `contact_collection_agent`.

### Summarizer behavior
After contact collection completion:
- `summariser_agent` creates distilled summary.
- If critical discovery unknowns remain after follow-up limits, include `unknowns` section.
- Do not include question lineage in final narrative output.
- Handoff to `email_agent` remains unchanged.

## Tracer-Bullet Implementation Phases

## Phase 1: Persist Discovery Tracking Contract
### What to build
Introduce schema and repository/tool changes required for batched discovery, follow-up linkage, round tracking, and explicit lead-level discovery state.

### Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Presale schema supports Initial vs FollowUp and round number.
- [ ] Follow-up records can link to parent initial question.
- [ ] Lead-level discovery counters/state persist and round-trip through MCP tools.
- [ ] Existing session data migrates safely with defaults.
- [ ] Serialization remains cycle-safe.

---

## Phase 2: Batched Initial Discovery Prompting
### What to build
Replace one-at-a-time discovery prompting with up-to-5 batched initial questions using non-overlapping topic selection and session-scoped dedup.

### Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Discovery asks up to 5 initial questions in one message.
- [ ] Asked-log records are persisted before response arrives.
- [ ] No semantically overlapping questions inside the same batch.
- [ ] Numbered response template is always provided.
- [ ] Initial asked count never exceeds 5.

---

## Phase 3: Deterministic Reply Parsing and Multi-Mapping
### What to build
Parse user replies in numbered format and allow one reply item to satisfy multiple discovery items/questions.

Status update (2026-03-30):
- Deterministic transition helper implemented in MCP (`evaluate_discovery_transition`) and wired into discovery instructions.
- Persisted deterministic fallback counters at lead level (`ConfirmationPromptCount`, `ConsecutivePartialResponseCount`).
- Deterministic parser MCP tool implemented (`parse_discovery_reply`) with numbered parsing and multi-map output schema.
- Discovery instructions now require `parse_discovery_reply` before writing discovery records.
- Agent-side deterministic helper implemented (`BuildAddDiscoveryQuestionPayloads`) to auto-construct `add_discovery_question_with_limit` payloads from parser output.
- Phase 3 core objective completed: deterministic transition + deterministic parsing/mapping source for discovery reply interpretation.

### Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Parser maps numbered user replies to asked questions reliably.
- [ ] One answer can map to multiple discovery records.
- [ ] Unresolved items are recomputed after each reply.
- [ ] Discovery completeness score is recomputed and persisted after each reply.
- [ ] RequestType refinement runs after each reply.
- [ ] Extra user info is persisted to additional-notes field(s).

---

## Phase 4: Follow-Up Rounds and Completion Gate
### What to build
Implement follow-up generation constrained by unresolved items, total follow-up cap, and round cap, with explicit user confirmation gating discovery completion.

Status update (2026-03-30):
- Deterministic follow-up policy service implemented in MCP (`evaluate_followup_policy`).
- Service enforces unresolved-topic targeting, follow-up total cap, follow-up round cap, and parent initial-question linkage.
- Service marks `unknowns_required` in completeness reason when limits are reached with unresolved critical gaps.
- Discovery workflow instructions now require `evaluate_followup_policy` before asking any follow-up questions.
- Atomic MCP operation implemented (`evaluate_and_persist_followups`) so approved follow-up asked-question records and lead counters are persisted in one transaction.
- Discovery workflow instructions now prefer `evaluate_and_persist_followups` to prevent prompt drift between approval and persistence.

### Acceptance criteria
- [x] Follow-up questions only target unresolved topics.
- [x] Follow-up question total never exceeds 5.
- [x] Follow-up rounds never exceed 2.
- [x] Every follow-up is linked to a parent initial question.
- [x] Follow-up prioritization uses unresolved topics plus internal completeness diagnostics.
- [x] Discovery completion requires explicit user confirmation.
- [x] Exhausted rounds with unresolved critical gaps are marked for summarizer unknowns.

---

## Phase 5: Handoff, Summary, and Export Alignment
### What to build
Align triage/discovery/contact/summarizer handoffs with the new discovery state and preserve existing ContactCollection and Email behavior.

Status update (2026-03-30):
- Triage routing now uses persisted discovery state first, including explicit confirmation and follow-up exhaustion signals.
- ContactCollection remains in the workflow with required-field validation and batch collection behavior.
- Summarizer now uses deterministic MCP policy evaluation (`evaluate_summary_composition`) before drafting output.
- Summary composition enforces distilled narrative mode and conditionally includes an `Unknowns` section from persisted diagnostics.
- Unknowns inclusion is tunable through a completeness threshold without exposing diagnostics to end users.
- Email/export behavior remains aligned with the updated summary flow and full regression tests remain green.

### Acceptance criteria
- [x] Triage routes discovery based on explicit discovery state (not only Q&A count).
- [x] ContactCollection step remains in the workflow and still validates required fields.
- [x] Summarizer emits distilled findings only.
- [x] Summarizer unknowns threshold is tunable using diagnostics (including completeness score), without exposing score to users.
- [x] Unknowns section appears when required by unresolved critical gaps.
- [x] Email export behavior remains unchanged except for consuming updated summary content.

---

## Phase 6: Tests and Regression Safety
### What to build
Add coverage for batched discovery, follow-up constraints, parent linkage, parser determinism, and end-to-end phase transitions.

Status update (2026-03-30):
- Added deterministic initial-batch non-overlap coverage at the agent helper boundary via `BuildInitialDiscoveryBatch`, including batch-cap enforcement.
- Added/updated tests for follow-up caps, parent-child linkage, and one-answer-to-many-question mapping coverage.
- Extended lifecycle integration coverage through discovery transition to contact collection, summarizer policy evaluation, summary persistence, and export idempotency checks (summarizer -> email path).
- Regression validation completed with green focused tests and full suite runs (`dotnet test tests/InterviewCoach.Agent.Tests/InterviewCoach.Agent.Tests.csproj` and `dotnet test InterviewCoach.slnx`).

### Acceptance criteria
- [x] Unit tests cover initial batch limits and non-overlap.
- [x] Unit tests cover follow-up total cap and round cap.
- [x] Unit tests validate parent-child linkage for follow-ups.
- [x] Unit tests validate one-answer-to-many-questions mapping.
- [x] Integration tests validate full lifecycle:
  Introduction -> Discovery (batched + follow-ups) -> ContactCollection -> Summarizer -> Email.
- [x] Existing behavior not targeted by this change has no regressions.

## Open Clarification to Resolve During Implementation
- Your decisions state "transition to summariser" with discovery completion gates while also keeping ContactCollection and Email. This plan interprets that as:
  - Discovery completion gates transition from Discovery to ContactCollection.
  - Summarizer still executes after ContactCollection completion.

## Risks and Knock-on Effects
- Relying on Q&A count alone for triage will be insufficient after batched prompts; explicit discovery state is required.
- Follow-up parent linking introduces referential integrity concerns in migrations and deletes.
- Deterministic numbered parsing improves reliability but requires prompt discipline and re-prompt handling.
- Keeping incomplete asked logs increases auditability but requires filtering logic when generating summaries.
- Strict confirmation without anti-stall fallback can block ContactCollection and downstream summary/export; fallback path is required and now defined above.

## Suggested Objective-Linked Improvement
Implemented in this plan: discovery completeness score is now a core internal diagnostic requirement.
